chapter_title.ar: باب الْبُكَاءِ وَ الدُّعَاءِ فِي الصَّلَاةِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 17 – The weeping and the supplication during the Salāt

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/81)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/81)
text: كُلُّ مَا كَلَّمْتَ اللَّهَ بِهِ فِي صَلَاةِ الْفَرِيضَةِ فَلَا بَأْسَ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, form Hammad Bin Isa, from one of his companions,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘Everything what you speak to Allahazwj with during an Obligatory Salāt, so there is no problem’.
en.sarwar: 5. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from Hammad ibn ‘Isa from certain persons of his people who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘Whatever (words) with which you speak to Allah in Salah (prayer) is not harmful.’” (Ahadith 4-5 of this Chapter are best explained by fatwas.)
